summary: Full e2e testing of DineSoulPage refactoring: DineCategoryStrip centering, TabBar amber pill style, 'How would [petName] love to eat?' heading, TummyProfile MIRA KNOWS vs GROW SOUL states, SoulQuestionCardDine with dine context, answer submission, soul score updates. All 10 feature checks PASSED. 12/12 backend tests PASSED.

minor.issue: 'dine' context not specifically handled in backend — falls through to default weight-based sorting (unlike 'celebrate' which has special prioritization). Food questions still appear due to diversity algorithm picking from Taste & Treat World folder. Works correctly in practice but lacks explicit dine-priority logic.

ui_bugs.issue: DINE_DIMS badge values hardcoded with 'Mojo' name: badge:'Mojo\'s body needs this' (meals) and badge:'Mojo loves these' (treats). When viewing for other pets (e.g. TestScoring), the dimension tiles still show 'Mojo\'s body needs this'. Fix: use t(dim.badge, petData.name) in render.

integration_issues.issue: Console error '[DineSoulPage] products fetch error: TypeError: Failed to fetch' appears on load. API endpoint /api/admin/pillar-products?pillar=dine&limit=600 works fine from backend (560 products). Likely a browser/CORS transient issue in preview env. Page gracefully falls back to hardcoded DINE_DIMS catalog.

issues: Minor: DineHeroV2 component defined in DineSoulPage.jsx (lines 885-927) but never used — dead code. Page correctly uses imported DineHero from components/dine/DineHero.
Category strip items: 'Treats & Rewa...' and 'Homemade & ...' get ellipsis-truncated — by design with textOverflow:ellipsis in 82px tiles.

action_items: Fix DINE_DIMS badge hardcoded 'Mojo' references — use t(dim.badge, petData.name) substitution in the dimension card render (currently dim.sub and dim.mira use t() but dim.badge does not)
Optional: Remove unused DineHeroV2 component from DineSoulPage.jsx (dead code, 42 lines)
Optional: Add dine-specific context prioritization to quick-questions backend endpoint to prioritize food/nutrition-related questions when context=dine (similar to celebrate's implementation)
critical_code_review_comments: DineHeroV2 defined in DineSoulPage.jsx but never used — the page imports and uses components/dine/DineHero instead. This is 42 lines of dead code.
DINE_DIMS: badge fields hardcoded with 'Mojo' string — should be dynamic (petName). Only dim.sub and dim.mira use t() substitution but badge does not.
TummyProfile GROW SOUL state correctly dispatches 'soulScoreUpdated' custom event after answer — confirmed working.
TummyProfile MIRA KNOWS state: shows 4-cell grid (LOVES/AVOID/NUTRITION GOAL/HEALTH NOTE) plus GROW SOUL section if unanswered questions exist — confirmed working.
SoulQuestionCardDine amber theme: dark amber background (#3d1200 → #7a2800), amber option buttons, amber submit button — amber theming verified.

quick-questions with context=dine works but has no dine-specific food-prioritization logic in backend. The celebrate context has explicit priority rules but dine does not — all questions sorted by weight only.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all features working, 2 minor bugs: hardcoded Mojo badge, dead code DineHeroV2)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing pets in DB

features_verified.dine_category_strip_inside_max_w_5xl: ✓ PASS — x=480, width=960 on 1920px viewport (not full-bleed)
features_verified.tabbar_amber_pill_style_centered: ✓ PASS — active tab: linear-gradient(135deg,#FF8C42,#C44400), inactive: #FFF3E0, borderRadius:20, display:flex justifyContent:center
features_verified.how_would_heading_section: ✓ PASS — 'How would Mojo love to eat?' visible with data-testid='dine-how-would-section'
features_verified.tummy_profile_mira_knows_state: ✓ PASS — Mojo (has allergies+loves): shows 'Mira knows Mojo's food preferences', 4-cell grid (LOVES/AVOID/NUTRITION GOAL/HEALTH NOTE)
features_verified.tummy_profile_grow_soul_state: ✓ PASS — TestScoring (no food data): shows '✦ GROW TESTSCORING'S TUMMY PROFILE', 4 soul question cards rendered
features_verified.soul_question_card_dine_questions: ✓ PASS — 4 questions fetched from /api/pet-soul/profile/{id}/quick-questions?limit=4&context=dine: Identity & Temperament(+5), Rhythm & Routine(+5), Taste & Treat World(+5 food!), Long Horizon(Health)(+5)
features_verified.soul_question_answer_submission: ✓ PASS — Clicking option then submit calls /api/pet-soul/profile/{id}/answer, score updates 0%→3%, question count 4→3, '+1 answered this session' badge shown
features_verified.soul_score_updated_event: ✓ PASS — soulScoreUpdated custom event dispatched on answer, DineSoulPage main component listener updates hero soul score badge
features_verified.eat_nourish_tab: ✓ PASS — Shows TummyProfile + 5-dimension grid + Mira Meal Pick + Guided Nutrition Paths
features_verified.dine_out_tab: ✓ PASS — Shows Pet-friendly spots grid with 6 venue cards
features_verified.no_pet_state: ✓ PASS — 'Add your dog to begin' button shows for unauthenticated users (data-testid='dine-add-pet-btn')

context_for_next_testing_agent: All DineSoulPage refactoring features verified working: (1) DineCategoryStrip inside max-w-5xl container, (2) TabBar amber pill buttons center-aligned, (3) 'How would [petName] love to eat?' heading with data-testid, (4) TummyProfile MIRA KNOWS state for Mojo (chicken allergy, Salmon/PeanutButter loves), (5) TummyProfile GROW SOUL state for TestScoring (no food data), (6) SoulQuestionCardDine renders 4 dine-context questions, (7) Answer submission works updating score 0%→3%. Two minor issues: (a) DINE_DIMS badge hardcoded 'Mojo' strings, (b) DineHeroV2 dead code in DineSoulPage.jsx. Auth: use dipali@clubconcierge.in JWT with secret 'tdb_super_secret_key_2025_woof'. Pet switch via window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('petChanged', {detail: petObj})) works for testing different pets.
